2605.18563.title: Readers make targeted regressions to plausible errors in reanalysis of "noisy-channel garden-path" sentences
2605.18563.paper_summary: A key question in psycholinguistics is how inferences about the meaning of linguistic input unfold incrementally a comprehender's mind. In this work, we study reading dynamics for ``noisy-channel garden-path'' sentences, which temporarily appear well-formed but feature late-appearing violations of expectation that can be resolved not by inferring an alternative syntactic structure, but by inferring the presence of an error. We find evidence for targeted regressions -- eye movements towards regions that are promising loci of possible errors in light of later-arriving information, showing patterns consistent with the posterior inferences of a model of noisy-channel processing with reanalysis. We discuss the implications of these findings for theories of noisy-channel language comprehension and information-theoretic explanations of reading dynamics.
2605.18563.paper_summary_zh: 一個心理語言學中的關鍵問題是，對語言輸入意義的推斷如何在理解者的心中逐步展開。<lb/>在這項研究中，我們探討了“噪聲通道花園小徑”句子的閱讀動態，這些句子暫時看起來結構良好，但特徵是後期出現的期望違規，這些違規無法通過推斷替代的句法結構來解決，而是通過推斷錯誤的存在來解決。<lb/>我們發現針對性回溯的證據——眼動向可能的錯誤位置的移動，這些位置在後來到達的信息的背景下顯示出有希望的特徵，顯示出與噪聲通道處理模型的後驗推斷一致的模式。<lb/>我們討論了這些發現對噪聲通道語言理解理論和閱讀動態的信息理論解釋的影響。
2605.18563.author: Thomas Hikaru Clark et.al.
2605.18563.authors: Thomas Hikaru Clark, Roger Levy, Edward Gibson
